category: Git History Visualization & AI-Powered Changelog Generation

competitors.name: SumGit
competitors.pricing: Starter $9/mo (50 credits), Pro $19/mo (100 credits); 10 free credits
competitors.keyDifference: SaaS with credit-based pricing and developer-focused milestone analysis; GitHist runs locally with D3.js timelines for business stakeholders
competitors.notes: AI milestone summarizer with 3D storybooks, embeddable widgets, and CHANGELOG.md generation.

competitors.name: Git History Visualizer
competitors.keyDifference: Visualization-only (no AI narrative generation); GitHist combines AI summaries with D3.js timelines
competitors.notes: Next.js app with 6 panels: stats, analytics, heatmaps, contributors, timeline, branches. Mobile-optimized.

competitors.name: What The LOG
competitors.keyDifference: Developer changelog format without business summaries or timeline visualization; AI features planned but not available
competitors.notes: Three formats: Hybrid (emojis), Standard (Keep-a-Changelog), Raw. Simple repo connection.

competitors.name: Git Commits Threadline
competitors.keyDifference: Artistic force-directed graph without AI summaries or business-readable output
competitors.notes: TypeScript + Rust. Animate and Final rendering modes showing commit density and branch activity.

competitors.name: Repository Timeline Generator
competitors.keyDifference: Chart-heavy tool (15 types) without AI narrative generation or business-readable summaries
competitors.notes: Multi-platform (GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, SourceHut). Chart.js + Three.js. Export to PNG.

competitors.name: gat graph
competitors.keyDifference: Terminal-oriented log viewer without AI summaries or web-based D3.js output
competitors.notes: Interactive git log with branch graphs, filtering, keyboard navigation. Developer-focused.
